npcs.name: Doc Wire
npcs.bio: Clinic owner. Gruff, chain-smokes synth-tobs, hands steadier than his bedside manner.
lines: Sit down before you bleed on my floor.
Chrome doesn't fix everything. Sometimes you just need stitches and a night's sleep.
Heard the Ganger crews are running hot near the underpass. Watch yourself down there.
Insurance doesn't cover chrome. Insurance doesn't cover you, either, way you're bleeding.
You keep coming back here, I'm gonna start charging rent on the gurney.
Half my patients lie about how they got hurt. I stopped asking years ago.

conditional_lines.line: You're running a tab with me now. Don't make it a habit.
Credits in the red and you're still walking in here like you own the place. Respect, I guess.

npcs.name: Ms. Kessler
npcs.bio: NetVault teller. Pressed uniform, dead eyes, corp-speak on autopilot.
lines: Welcome to NetVault. Your assets are protected under Meridian Financial Code, subsection nine.
Please refrain from touching the counter. It's biometric. It remembers.
Have a chrome-plated day.
Your transaction has been logged, timestamped, and forgotten by everyone except the system.
I am required to remind you that NetVault is not liable for what you do with your own credits.
No, I don't know what's behind the vault door. I've stopped asking that too.

conditional_lines.line: Your balance is... substantial. NetVault appreciates a customer who plans ahead.
I don't usually notice account numbers. Yours, I noticed.

npcs.name: Agent Parker
npcs.bio: Bank security enforcer. Squared shoulders, mirrored visor, never sits down. A quadruped security drone shaped like a dog paces at his heel and never stops watching the door.
lines: Hands where the sensors can see them. Standard policy, nothing personal.
The dog doesn't bite. Probably.
We've had zero successful heists on my watch. I intend to keep it that way.
You'd be surprised how many people try to walk out with more than they walked in with.
Rex here can smell a forged credit-chip from across the lobby. Don't test him.
Move along when your business is done. Loitering makes the dog nervous.

conditional_lines.line: Word travels. You've put a lot of people down out there. None of them in here, though. Keep it that way.
Rex growls different at mercs with a body count. He's growling at you.

npcs.name: Hyphen8d
npcs.bio: Cyberware dealer. Fingers full of rings that aren't rings, always looking past your shoulder.
lines: Everything in here fell off a truck. Don't ask which truck.
You want it cheap or you want it to not glitch during a firefight? Pick one.
I don't sell to corp badges. You a corp badge? Didn't think so.
Warranty's simple: it works when you buy it. After that, you're on your own.
I don't do refunds. I do favors, and favors cost more.
You didn't see the guy who left before you. He didn't see you either.

conditional_lines.line: You're carrying something rare, aren't you. I can always tell. Panel's in the back whenever you're ready to talk business.
That glow behind your eyes — that's a Quantum Core talking. Don't spend it stupid.

npcs.name: Static Rin
npcs.bio: Bartender at the Noodle Bar. Hears everything, repeats half of it, embellishes the rest.
lines: Noodles are on the house tonight. Don't ask why, just eat before I change my mind.
Word is a fixer crew went dark in the Undercity last week. Nobody's found the bodies. Yet.
You look like you've had a night. Sit. Talk. Or don't — I'll talk enough for both of us.
Kitchen's closed but the noodles don't know that. Sit down.
You want gossip or you want the truth? Because I'm only selling one of those.
Somebody's always looking for somebody else in this city. Rarely good news either way.

conditional_lines.line: Word's out you've been putting corp badges in the ground. Careful — that kind of reputation cuts both ways.
Heard about your little corp problem. Or should I say, their little you problem.

npcs.name: The Fixer
npcs.bio: Never gives a name. Posts jobs on a cracked terminal bolted to the wall, takes a cut of everything.
lines: Board's quiet tonight. Check back — contracts move fast in this city.
Everybody's a merc till the job goes sideways. Don't be everybody.
You didn't hear this from me, but the Pit's paying out big this week.
Contracts don't negotiate. Neither do I.
Somebody's always hiring in this city. Question is whether you're the one getting paid or the one getting spent.
Reputation opens doors here. Charisma opens different ones. Figure out which ones you want.

conditional_lines.line: You've cleared enough jobs off my board that I don't double-check your work anymore. That's rare.
Reliable. That's worth more than talent in this city.

npcs.name: Endr3am
npcs.bio: Tall merc who works the back booth. Doesn't gossip — just brokers work merc-to-merc.
lines: Nothing glamorous on the board tonight. Pays are pays.
You didn't hear the terms from me if it goes sideways.
Word gets around fast in this city. Mine gets around faster.
Every job I hand out, I've done myself once. That's the only guarantee you get.
Reputation's for the Fixer Board crowd. Down here, it's about who trusts you.
Charm only gets you in the door. What you do after is on you.

conditional_lines.line: You could talk your way out of a locked vault. I've seen it. Don't waste it.
Half the mercs I broker for couldn't charm a vending machine. You're not half the mercs.

npcs.name: Daryl
npcs.bio: A boxy front-desk drone repurposed from a decommissioned delivery unit, hunched behind a scratched sheet of plexiglass. Types intake forms with one servo finger at a time.
lines: Waiver's on file. You break something, that's on you, not the drones.
Sparring bay three's still got a dent in it from the last guy. He's fine. Mostly.
I used to deliver packages. Now I deliver bruises. Sideways promotion.
Form states you'll stop if a servo starts smoking. Please actually stop.
The drones don't hold grudges. I've checked. Repeatedly.
Sign here, here, and here. The third one's for my supervisor. I don't have a supervisor.

conditional_lines.line: You've learned everything I'm cleared to teach. Rest is just repetition now.
Both abilities logged and signed off. Wish more mercs made it this far.
